title: trifolium gemellum
intro: Trifolium gemellum, also known as twin-leaved clover or gemellum, is a species of clover in the pea family Fabaceae. It is a perennial plant with two-lobed leaves that are characteristic of the species. The medicinal properties of Trifolium gemellum include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of consuming Trifolium gemellum include its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels, and it has also been used to treat digestive issues and as a natural remedy for colds and flu.
